course_name_original: CS 290 Special Topics in Applied Computer Science
course_name_english: Special Topics in Applied Computer Science

course_description: First-year seminar introducing university learning skills and academic orientation.
Continuation of freshman seminar focusing on academic development and communication skills.
Study of Armenian language structure and literary works.
Continuation of Armenian literature and language analysis.
Historical overview of Armenian civilization and society.
Advanced study of Armenian historical developments and institutions.
Arts and humanities elective courses developing cultural literacy and interpretation skills.
Social science electives examining societal and economic systems.
Quantitative reasoning courses emphasizing mathematical thinking.
Differential calculus and functions for quantitative analysis.
Integral calculus and advanced mathematical modeling concepts.
Multivariable calculus and vector-based mathematical analysis.
Matrix operations, vector spaces, and linear transformations.
Mathematical structures used in computing such as graphs and logic.
Fundamental probability theory and stochastic modeling.
Descriptive statistics, inference, and statistical modeling basics.
Advanced statistical methods for data analysis.
Computational methods for solving mathematical problems numerically.
Overview of computing concepts, programming, and algorithms.
Programming tools and techniques for data analysis and modeling.

course_name_original: DS115 Data Structures/Algorithms in Data Science
course_name_english: Data Structures and Algorithms in Data Science
course_description: Core algorithms and data structures used in analytical computing.
Database design, SQL, and data management for large datasets.
Visual representation of data using modern analytical tools.
Supervised and unsupervised machine learning algorithms and applications.
AI methods including search, reasoning, and intelligent systems.

course_name_original: DS150 Physics & Chemistry in Life Science
course_name_english: Physics and Chemistry in Life Sciences
course_description: Scientific foundations relevant to biological data analysis.
Biological systems and molecular mechanisms used in bioinformatics.
Data analysis tools for decision making in business environments.
Statistical models for temporal data and forecasting methods.
Computational methods for analyzing biological data.
Algorithms and computational models applied to biological systems.
Modeling biological systems using network-based approaches.
Final project applying data science methods to real-world problems.
Foundations of computation, automata theory, and complexity.
Software modeling and design using object-oriented methodologies.
Advanced algorithmic techniques and complexity analysis.
Mathematical and architectural foundations of network communication.
Encryption algorithms, cryptographic protocols, and security principles.
Rendering, modeling, and graphical algorithms.
Software development lifecycle, project management, and quality assurance.
Advanced OOP patterns and scalable software design.
Database design, indexing, and large-scale data management.
Process management, memory management, and system architecture.
Development of network applications and communication protocols.
Parsing, lexical analysis, and compiler construction.
Security threats, system vulnerabilities, and defensive techniques.
Statistical learning models and predictive algorithms.
Neural networks, deep architectures, and representation learning.
Data analysis pipelines and large-scale analytics methods.
Techniques for visual exploration and presentation of data.
Computational analysis of biological data and genomic information.
Intelligent systems, reasoning, and machine perception.
Planning and managing complex software development projects.
Innovation, startup development, and technology commercialization.
Statistical modeling and forecasting of temporal data.
Digital image analysis, filtering, and pattern extraction.
Applied industry project integrating program knowledge.

course_name_original: CS392 Special Topics in Computer Science: Distributed Algorithms
course_description: Advanced distributed computing algorithms and systems.
Preparation for thesis or capstone research project.
Final research thesis writing and defense preparation.
Economic analysis methods for engineering decision making and project evaluation.
Advanced optimization and analytical modeling techniques for operational systems.

course_name_original: IESM 301 Analysis and Design of Data Systems
course_name_english: Analysis and Design of Data Systems
course_description: Methods for designing and managing data systems for enterprise decision support.
Quality control, process improvement, and management methodologies.
Modeling and simulation of complex industrial systems and processes.
Analytical tools for evaluating and optimizing production systems.
Preparation phase for the final capstone research or industry project.
Research-based thesis addressing industrial engineering problems.
Applied project integrating systems engineering knowledge.

course_name_original: IESM 351 Sustainable Smart and Resource Efficient Systems 1
course_name_english: Sustainable Smart and Resource Efficient Systems I
course_description: Sustainable technologies and resource-efficient system design.

course_name_original: IESM 352 Sustainable Smart and Resource Efficient Systems 2
course_name_english: Sustainable Smart and Resource Efficient Systems II
course_description: Decision-making tools for sustainable engineering systems.
Innovation management and technology entrepreneurship.
Technologies and systems for renewable energy and sustainability.
Machine learning and predictive modeling methods for data analysis.

course_name_original: IESM 315 Design and Analysis of Experiments
course_description: Statistical experimental design and analysis techniques.

course_name_original: IESM 347 Design and Innovation of Information Services
course_name_english: Design and Innovation of Information Services
course_description: Innovation and design of modern information services and systems.
Management of logistics networks and supply chain operations.

course_name_original: IESM 349 Enabling Competitive Advantage through Information Technology
course_description: Strategic use of information technology for organizational advantage.
Leadership and management of engineering projects and technology teams.

course_name_original: IESM 390 Integrative Project in Modern Production
course_description: Integrated analysis of modern manufacturing systems.
Digital design tools and CAD modeling techniques.
Automated manufacturing technologies and CAM systems.
Advanced computer-aided engineering and manufacturing integration.
